concepts.finding: Deep compression pipeline (pruning + trained quantization + Huffman coding) reduces neural network storage by 35-49x without accuracy loss: AlexNet 240MB to 6.9MB, VGG-16 552MB to 11.3MB.
concepts.implication: Aggressive compression enables deploying large ML models in resource-constrained environments, applicable to on-device search models for mobile query understanding.
concepts.open_question: Can similar compression ratios be achieved for transformer-based ranking models without significant accuracy degradation?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A Deep Compression pipeline (pruning + 8-bit quantization + Huffman) will reduce BERT-base ranking model size by 30x while maintaining within 2% of original NDCG@10.
